flags.concern: Conflict resolution: Recurring debt: the retries-exhausted fallback would silently discard dirty local edits, which breaks the stated last-write-wins guarantee.
flags.evidence: In /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:375-405, polled server data is only accepted once savedSeqRef >= editSeqRef, and then it overwrites local state with commitData(..., { save: false, skipHistory: true, updateLastSavedSignature: true }). Step 3 proposes forcing savedSeqRef.current = editSeqRef.current after failed retries or a failed version refresh, so the next poll will replace dataRef.current with server state. That contradicts the existing UX contract in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:451-461 ('Keep local draft' vs 'Discard and reload') and the idea's requirement that local edits eventually save.

flags.concern: Timeline save queue: the pending-save drain is underspecified and can resubmit the same snapshot after the conflict retry already saved it, causing avoidable extra version bumps and new conflicts.
flags.evidence: The current hook keeps dataRef.current synced to the newest local state in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:85-89 and :217-218. Step 2 retries with saveTimeline(dataRef.current, editSeqRef.current), so that retry can already include edits queued during the failed save. Step 4 then says to drain pendingSaveRef after success. Without an explicit seq/signature dedupe rule, the plan can save the same latest snapshot twice, unnecessarily bumping config_version and recreating the conflict window it is trying to shrink.

flags.concern: Timeline save queue: Over-engineering: the plan adds a second ad-hoc serialization mechanism instead of reusing the repository's existing serialized timeline write queue.
flags.evidence: The repo already has a tested per-entity write serializer in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/lib/timelineWriteQueue.ts:170-245, with coverage in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/lib/__tests__/timelineWriteQueue.test.ts:63-177. Step 1 proposes new isSavingRef/pendingSaveRef queue state inside useTimelineSave.ts rather than building on that shared primitive, which increases custom concurrency logic in an already fragile area.

flags.concern: Timeline save testing: the plan leaves a concurrency state-machine change to manual testing even though this repository already has the automated test scaffolding to cover it.
flags.evidence: There is already save-related Vitest coverage in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.test.ts:1-239, and a reusable hook test wrapper in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/test/test-utils.tsx:1-82. The proposed changes touch timers, refs, React Query mutation callbacks, and polling gates in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts, but Step 6 only specifies manual checks.
